rto_compliance.notes: Restore completed within RTO but with only 10-minute margin. Database restore phase took 136 minutes, up from 112 minutes in the Q3 test, reflecting data growth.

application_validation.details: All critical application functions validated successfully against restored database.

previous_quarter_comparison.trend_analysis: Restore duration increased by 35 minutes (18%) quarter-over-quarter. Database restore phase increased by 24 minutes (21.4%). Data volume increased by 16.9% (138.5 GB to 161.9 GB). If this trend continues, next quarter's restore may approach or exceed the 4-hour RTO.

issues_encountered.description: S3 download throughput from us-west-2 was approximately 580 MB/s, below the expected 800 MB/s baseline. Likely due to concurrent cross-region traffic.
issues_encountered.impact: Added approximately 8 minutes to the download phase
issues_encountered.resolution: No immediate action taken. Recommend investigating dedicated transfer acceleration for DR scenarios.

recommendations: Monitor restore time trend closely -- current trajectory suggests potential RTO breach within 1-2 quarters
Evaluate parallel restore strategies to offset data growth impact
Consider dedicated S3 Transfer Acceleration for DR backup downloads
Investigate database partitioning to enable parallel table restoration

vp_infrastructure.name: Marcus Chen
vp_infrastructure.signature: Approved -- noting RTO margin concern for Q1 2026 planning
